content: Just as the high school English writing workshop framework teaches human learners to structure explicit verbal reflection on their own drafted writing to identify gaps, LLM self-reflection agents can borrow this structured pedagogical design to train iterative, human-aligned self-correction instead of relying only on inconsistent implicit problem-solving contrasts. This structured reflection practice turns unguided self-improvement into a repeatable, teachable skill for autonomous agents, much like it does for student writers.
